summary:
This file is a setup template that tells the system how to run and where to store its working files.

What it controls:
- Where the tool keeps its internal data: `./.diffdoc`
- Which AI service to use: local on your machine or a cloud service
- Which AI models to use for writing summaries and creating embeddings
- Where to send requests if using a local AI server or OpenAI
- How much work to do at once
- Which files to include or skip
- Which ignore file to read

Business behavior:
- It does not process user content by itself.
- It defines the operating rules for the tool before any work starts.
- It lets a team switch between local processing and cloud processing without changing the main application.

User impact:
- If configured for local use, data stays on the machine or internal server.
- If configured for cloud use, content may be sent to an external AI service.
- Performance can change based on batch size and concurrency settings.
- File selection rules affect which project files are analyzed and which are ignored.

Visible outcomes:
- The tool will store its output in the `.diffdoc` folder.
- It will use the chosen AI model for summaries and another model for text matching/search.
- It will respect file include/exclude rules and ignore lists.
- If the API key is left blank, cloud access will not work until a key is provided.

In short, this is a configuration example that sets the tool’s storage location, AI provider, model choices, processing speed, and file filtering rules.
